Tooltip_SelfOcclusion: Calculates shadows cast by the mesh onto itself. Useful for objects with complex geometry such as clothing or accessories.
Tooltip_MutualOcclusion: Calculates shadows cast by the registered occluder meshes onto the target. Enable this when you want other objects to cast shadows onto the target.
Tooltip_LowResource: Enable this if Unity crashes or your PC becomes unstable during baking. It is slower but much safer.
Help_LowResource: Low Resource Mode: Processes smaller chunks to stay stable. Baking time will be significantly longer.
Tooltip_RayCount: Number of shadow rays cast per texel. Higher values produce smoother, more accurate shadows at the cost of longer bake times.
Tooltip_MaxDistance: How far shadow rays can travel. Larger values produce wider, softer shadows. Smaller values give tighter, more localized shadows.
Tooltip_AOBias: Offset distance from the surface to start shadow rays. Increase if you see black noise (self-shadowing), decrease if shadows detach from contact points.
Tooltip_Iterations: How much to clean up grainy shadows. Higher values look smoother but might blur fine details.
Tooltip_SigmaPos: Keeps the smoothing aligned with the object's shape. Lower values keep corners and edges sharper.
Tooltip_SigmaNrm: Ensures smoothing follows surface curves. Higher values keep curved surfaces looking clean.
Tooltip_SigmaLum: Controls how much of the original shadow contrast is kept. Lower values result in bolder shadows.
Tooltip_Mode: Choose between detecting general edge wear or specific convex/concave shapes.
Tooltip_Strength: How bold the detected edges should be. Higher values result in more contrast.
Tooltip_Bias: Adjusts the overall brightness. 0.5 means flat areas will be a neutral gray.
Tooltip_AddTarget: Drag and drop a GameObject here to add it as a bake target. Requires a MeshFilter or SkinnedMeshRenderer.
Tooltip_AddOccluder: Drag and drop a GameObject here to register it as an occluder. Occluders cast shadows onto the target but are not baked themselves.
Tooltip_ClearAll: Remove all registered occluder meshes at once.
Tooltip_OutputRes: The size of the final image. Larger images look sharper but take up more disk space.
Tooltip_UVChannel: The UV channel to use for baking. Auto selects the first valid channel. Specify explicitly when your mesh uses UV1 for lightmap or AO UVs.
Tooltip_OutputFolder: Where to save the result. If left empty, it will be saved next to the original model.
Tooltip_Overwrite: If disabled, a new numbered file will be created instead of replacing the old one.
Tooltip_Dilation: Expands the texture slightly to fill in small gaps. Helps prevent unwanted lines appearing on the model.
Tooltip_ShadowColor: The color of the darkest areas. Defaults to black, but you can change it to fit your style.
Tooltip_GaussianBlur: Slightly blurs the final result for a softer look.
Tooltip_BlurPasses: How many times to apply the blur. More passes produce a softer effect.
Msg_NoMeshFound: No mesh found on {0}. Attach a MeshFilter or SkinnedMeshRenderer.
Help_UVChannelMissing: The selected UV channel is missing from some meshes. Please switch to Auto or check your mesh UVs.
Error_NoUVChannel: No valid UV channel found on mesh '{0}'. At least one UV set is required for AO baking.
Error_NotReadable: Mesh '{0}' is not readable. Enable 'Read/Write Enabled' in the import settings.
Warning_ChildMesh_Body:
The target '{0}' has its MeshFilter on a child object '{1}'.

This tool assumes the mesh is attached directly to the specified GameObject. Bake results may be positionally inaccurate.

For best results, specify '{1}' directly as the target.
Dialog_ReadWrite_Body:
'{0}' has Read/Write disabled.

AO baking requires read access to the mesh data. Enable it in the import settings now?
